name: Alarcón Reservoir Loop from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla
name_original: Embalse de Alarcón desde San Lorenzo de la Parilla — circular por Olivares de Júcar
description: Cycle 18.8 miles around Embalse de Alarcón from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla, passing Olivares de Júcar with castle views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mancha Alta offers routes through a diverse landscape characterized by extensive plains, vast vineyards, and river basins. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, with some areas presenting undulating or hilly sections, particularly towards the Serranía de Cuenca. This interprovincial region in Spain provides a variety of routes suitable for different skill levels, traversing agricultural lands and historical areas. Best touring cycling routes around Mancha Alta The most popular touring cycling route is Monasterio de Uclés y Parque arqueológico de Segóbriga — circular route, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete, exploring historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tarancón - Barajas de Melo Loop, a difficult 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path, offering a challenging loop through the countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Mancha Alta is defined by plains, vineyards, and river basins, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mancha Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Mancha Alta?
A: Mancha Alta offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails, allowing cyclists to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while touring cycling in Mancha Alta?
A: You'll experience a varied landscape characterized by extensive plains, vast vineyards, and river basins. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, though some areas, particularly towards the Serranía de Cuenca, present more undulating or hilly sections. You can also find routes passing through areas famous for their whitewashed windmills and expansive grape-growing regions.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mancha Alta?
A: Yes, Mancha Alta has several routes suitable for families or those looking for a gentler ride. There are 3 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for a relaxed exploration of the region's charming scenery. These routes offer a great way to enjoy the outdoors without significant challenges.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Mancha Alta?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Mancha Alta are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086158767/monasterio-de-ucles-y-parque-arqueologico-de-segobriga-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Uclés y Parque arqueológico de Segóbriga — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022696040/horcajo-de-santiago-corral-de-almaguer-villanueva-de-alcardete-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084532799/alarcon-reservoir-loop-from-san-lorenzo-de-la-parrilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir Loop from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or cultural attractions can I visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Mancha Alta is rich in history and culture. You can cycle past significant landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5917394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Uclés and Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the archaeological park of Segóbriga. The region is also dotted with medieval castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz&lt;/a&gt;, and charming villages with traditional architecture.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes offer scenic views of the region's water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045670/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-valverde-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt; takes you around the Alarcón Reservoir. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hito Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, a crucial stopover for migratory birds, or enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Mancha Alta?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mancha Alta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the region's quiet agricultural landscapes and historical points of interest.

Q: Can I cycle through areas related to Don Quixote?
A: Mancha Alta is famously the setting for Miguel de Cervantes' 'Don Quixote.' Many touring cycling routes allow you to follow in the footsteps of the knight-errant, passing through villages famous for their hilltop whitewashed windmills, such as those found in Consuegra and Campo de Criptana, which were immortalized in the novel.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mancha Alta?
A: The region offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in the plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Mancha Alta offers 13 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often feature more significant elevation changes and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086169310/tarancon-barajas-de-melo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarancón - Barajas de Melo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 41.4 km path with over 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural parks or unique natural features can I explore by bike?
A: While much of Mancha Alta is characterized by plains, you can find unique natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084296488/saelices-from-montalbo-circular-by-the-laguna-de-el-hito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saelices from Montalbo — circular by the Laguna de El Hito&lt;/a&gt; route takes you near the Laguna de El Hito, a significant wetland for migratory birds. The broader region also includes the stunning Ruidera Lakes Nature Reserve, a chain of fifteen lakes connected by waterfalls and streams, offering an oasis amidst the plains.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through vineyards?
A: Absolutely. Mancha Alta is renowned for its vast vineyards, particularly around Tomelloso. Cycling through these expansive grape-growing areas offers picturesque views and insights into the local wine production. Many routes traverse these agricultural landscapes, providing a unique cultural and scenic experience.
tips: Municipality located 50 km from Cuenca and 5 km from the Alarcón Reservoir. It has only 300 inhabitants. The surroundings are very beautiful and pedaling along the CM-2103 is a real pleasure.

The Alarcón reservoir on the bed of the Júcar river is a very picturesque and quiet place. In the heart of the interior of the province of Cuenca, it can be reached by various agricultural roads through a characteristic landscape of this area of La Mancha.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Embalse de Alarcón circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate for touring bicycles. It requires good fitness but is generally manageable, featuring gentle descents and ascents. While mostly paved, some unpaved sections might be encountered.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.2 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 317 meters. It is estimated to take around 1 hour and 54 minutes to complete by touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain features gentle descents and ascents, with sections utilizing bike lanes. Much of the trail is likely paved, but as is common for moderate routes, there could be unpaved segments. In some areas, especially near the Alarcón gorges, you might occasionally need to dismount.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular landscapes along the Júcar River and around the Embalse de Alarcón. Key highlights include panoramic views of the historic town of Alarcón, its impressive castle, and ancient walls. The route also passes through Olivares de Júcar and offers stunning Views of the Alarcón Reservoir.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from San Lorenzo de la Parrilla. You should be able to find parking within or near the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and some potentially unpaved sections, this route is best suited for families with older children or those who are comfortable with moderate cycling challenges. It's not explicitly described as 'easy' for all ages.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Alarcón circular cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is outdoors, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling route are not provided. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers ample shade in some areas, which can be particularly pleasant during warmer months. However, generally, spring and autumn provide the most comfortable temperatures for cycling in this region.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Olivares de Júcar and starts near San Lorenzo de la Parrilla. The historic town of Alarcón, with its castle, is also nearby. These towns are good places to look for cafes, pubs, or accommodation options.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of official trails such as Cañada Real Conquense - Cañada Real de los Chorrros (Ramal del saliente) and Cañada Real Conquense - Cañada Real de los Chorrros.
